---
applyTo: 'libs/grid/**'
---

# Grid Pitfalls

Counterintuitive behaviors and traps specific to the grid's DOM, rendering, and plugin system. **Check this list when something fails unexpectedly or "works but looks wrong."**

> Conventions and API guidance live in other instruction files. This file is strictly for gotchas.
> Architectural knowledge (how/why the system works, invariants, data flows, design rationale) belongs in `.github/knowledge/` files ‚Äî not here. Do not duplicate knowledge-file content as pitfalls.

1. **Plugin container access ‚Äî `children[0]` is unreliable** - Use `this.gridElement.querySelector('.tbw-grid-root')` to find the grid's root container. Light DOM children (e.g. `<tbw-grid-column>`) are re-appended before `.tbw-grid-root`, making `children[0]` unpredictable.
2. **Register external focus containers for overlays** - Custom editors that append elements to `<body>` (datepickers, dropdowns, color pickers) must call `grid.registerExternalFocusContainer(panel)` so the grid treats focus inside those elements as "still in the grid." Without registration, the grid will close the editor when focus moves to the overlay. Call `grid.unregisterExternalFocusContainer(panel)` when the overlay is destroyed. Angular's `BaseOverlayEditor` does this automatically.
3. **Column groups are fragmented, not merged** - `computeColumnGroups()` creates one entry per contiguous run of same-group columns, so a group with columns split across the grid produces multiple fragments. Use `mergeAdjacentSameIdGroups()` when you need the merged view for rendering. Pre-compute group merges once via `mergeGroups()` ‚Äî don't call `findEmbeddedImplicitGroups()` + `mergeAdjacentSameIdGroups()` separately in each function.
4. **Plugin `afterRender` ordering = registration order by default** - Plugins execute hooks in array order unless overridden by `manifest.hookPriority`. If your plugin's `afterRender` needs DOM created by another plugin's `afterRender`, either set a higher `hookPriority` value or defer with `queueMicrotask()` to run after all plugins' hooks complete. The same applies to `processColumns` ‚Äî PinnedColumnsPlugin uses `hookPriority: { processColumns: -10 }` so column reordering happens before other plugins (e.g., TreePlugin) wrap specific columns.
5. **Pinned columns split implicit group headers** - When a pinned column belongs to an implicit (unlabelled) group, `splitMixedPinImplicitGroup()` replaces the single group header cell with separate fragments at pin boundaries. The pinned fragment gets `sticky` positioning; the non-pinned utility-only remnant has its `border-right` suppressed so it visually merges with the adjacent explicit group.
6. **Touch scroll boundaries require `window.scrollBy`** - The grid sets `touch-action: none` on `.rows-viewport` and `.faux-vscroll`. Because this is evaluated at gesture start and cannot be changed mid-gesture, you **cannot** release scroll control back to the browser. Propagate surplus delta to the page via `window.scrollBy()`.
7. **`builtInSort` is public API ‚Äî must not mutate input** - `builtInSort` is exported and documented as non-mutating (`[...rows].sort()`). Internal callers bypass it via `sortInPlace()`. Gate internal fast paths with `handler === builtInSort` (reference equality) ‚Äî never string comparison or duck-typing.
8. **`applySorts` copies; `sortRowsInPlace` doesn't** - The public `applySorts()` creates a copy before sorting. Inside `MultiSortPlugin.processRows`, use `sortRowsInPlace()` instead ‚Äî the input is already a mutable copy from plugin-manager.
9. **Scroll-driven DOM state must survive re-renders and virtualization** - If a plugin maintains scroll-driven state (e.g., `translateX`, toggled classes based on `scrollLeft`), call the update logic from both `onScroll` and `afterRender` (re-renders rebuild the DOM). Additionally, per-cell visual state (e.g., `position: sticky`, offsets) must be applied in `afterCellRender` too ‚Äî `afterRender` doesn't run during scroll, and virtualization recycles row pool elements without those styles.
10. **Custom `renderRow` bypasses column-level features** - When a plugin's `renderRow` hook takes over cell creation (e.g., Pivot, GroupingRows), the grid's normal cell rendering pipeline is skipped. Column-level features like `format`, `cellRenderer`, and `cellClass` must be applied manually in the custom render code. Forgetting this silently loses formatting.
11. **Sort indicators need both `part` and `class` attributes** - Core CSS targets `[part~='sort-indicator']` for visibility/opacity; MultiSort CSS targets `.sort-indicator` for margin. Use `this.updateSortIndicator(cell, direction)` from `BaseGridPlugin` ‚Äî it handles both attributes, icon resolution, aria/data attributes, and correct DOM insertion. Never create sort indicators manually.
12. **`background: currentColor` must NOT be generic on `[data-icon]`** - Only apply it to icons that actually use SVG mask rendering (filter/filter-active). Applying it to all `[data-icon]:empty::before` creates solid colored blocks that obscure text/emoji icons. The mask `size/repeat/position` properties are safe to ship generically (no visible effect when mask-image is `none`).
13. **User-returned HTML strings must pass through `sanitizeHTML()`** ‚Äî Any path that writes a user-supplied string to `innerHTML` (icon config, `gridConfig.icons.*`, cell renderer string returns, group-header renderer string returns, light-DOM tool panel fallback content, plugin wrappers) must wrap the value with `sanitizeHTML()` from `core/internal/sanitize.ts`. `textContent` is wrong here ‚Äî it renders HTML/SVG icon strings as literal text. Raw `innerHTML` is wrong ‚Äî it's an XSS sink. The canonical reference is `core/internal/rows.ts` (cell renderer path). When adding a new renderer-like extension point, mirror that pattern exactly.
14. **Context menu CSS custom properties are not inherited** - `.tbw-context-menu` is appended to `document.body`, outside the `tbw-grid` element. CSS custom properties set on `tbw-grid` are NOT inherited. The `ContextMenuPlugin.copyGridStyles()` method bridges this gap by copying computed values as inline styles. When adding new icon or theme variables that the context menu uses, add them to `CSS_VARS_TO_COPY`.
15. **Cell `> *` overflow rule clips plugin wrapper elements** - Core CSS sets `.cell > * { overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis }` for text truncation. Plugin wrappers injected as cell children (e.g., `.tree-cell-wrapper`) inherit this clipping ‚Äî icons and indentation get cut off, especially in narrow sticky columns. Override with `overflow: visible` on the specific wrapper element.
16. **`requestColumnsRender()` vs `requestRender()` ‚Äî use minimal phase** - `requestRender()` triggers `RenderPhase.ROWS` (processRows + virtualization). `requestColumnsRender()` triggers `RenderPhase.COLUMNS` which includes processColumns + header rebuild. Using COLUMNS phase for incremental data updates (e.g., lazy page loads) causes the full pipeline to run, which can change scroll height and create scroll‚Üírender feedback loops. Only use `requestColumnsRender()` when column definitions actually change. For row data updates, `requestRender()` suffices ‚Äî but ensure `processColumns` wrapped the column during initial setup.
17. **`processColumns` wraps accumulate on repeated calls** - `processColumns` runs on every COLUMNS+ phase render. If a plugin wraps a column's `viewRenderer` (e.g., Tree wrapping with indent/toggle), it must cache the original unwrapped renderer and always wrap from that ‚Äî otherwise each call nests another wrapper layer, causing duplicate icons/indentation.
18. **Master-detail Set functions mutate in-place** - `toggleDetailRow`, `expandDetailRow`, and `collapseDetailRow` mutate the input `Set` and return it (same reference). This is intentional for O(1) performance. Do not rely on reference inequality (`result !== input`) to detect changes ‚Äî use the return value's contents instead.
19. **`emit()` ‚âÝ `emitPluginEvent()` ‚Äî wrong channel = silent bug** - `emit()` dispatches a DOM `CustomEvent` on the grid element (reaches external `addEventListener` consumers). `emitPluginEvent()` dispatches to the internal plugin event bus (reaches `this.on()` subscribers in other plugins). If a plugin uses `emit()` for an event that other plugins need to react to (e.g., `sort-change` for Selection invalidation), those plugins never hear it. Use `broadcast()` when both audiences need the event.
20. **Direct plugin access via `grid.plugins` namespace is fragile** - Never use `grid.plugins.clipboard.copy()` or similar direct property access. Use `grid.query('clipboard:copy')` instead. Direct access couples to the plugin's internal method names and breaks when plugins are renamed, not loaded, or restructured. The query system provides manifest-declared contracts that are validated at plugin registration.
21. **Query declarations must match query usage** - If a plugin calls `this.grid.query('someQuery')`, the plugin that handles it must declare `queries: [{ type: 'someQuery' }]` in its manifest, AND the calling plugin should declare a dependency (at least optional) on the handler. Orphaned queries (declared but never called, or called but never handled) are silent failures.
22. **`.header` is nested ‚Äî don't use it as an `insertBefore` reference inside `.tbw-scroll-area`** ‚Äî The grid's sticky `.header` lives at `.tbw-scroll-area > .rows-body-wrapper > .rows-body > .header`, NOT as a direct child of `.tbw-scroll-area`. A plugin that does `scrollArea.insertBefore(myWrapper, header.nextSibling)` throws `NotFoundError` because the reference node isn't a child of the container. To inject a wrapper at the top of the scroll area, use `scrollArea.insertBefore(myWrapper, scrollArea.firstChild)` (the sticky header still sits visually at the top because `position: sticky` is scoped to `.rows-body`). PinnedRowsPlugin's top-slot rendering hit this; tests didn't catch it because the spec mock placed `.header` directly under `.tbw-scroll-area`.
23. **Conditional row attributes in `afterRender` must clear on the negative branch** ‚Äî Virtualization recycles `.data-grid-row` DOM elements as the visible window changes (and as `flattenedRows` grows after expand/collapse). A plugin that does `if (cond) rowEl.setAttribute('aria-expanded', ‚Ä¶)` without an `else rowEl.removeAttribute(‚Ä¶)` will leak stale attributes onto rows that reuse a previously-true element. TreePlugin hit this with `aria-expanded` (issue #282) ‚Äî leaf child rows inherited `aria-expanded="true"` from a previously-expanded parent that occupied the same DOM slot. Same trap applies to any per-row `aria-*`, `data-*`, or class toggled in `afterRender` / `afterCellRender`.
24. **Grid-level `paste`/`keydown` handlers must skip editable targets** ‚Äî A plugin that calls `event.preventDefault()` on every paste/keydown reaching the `tbw-grid` element will swallow keystrokes the user intended for an active editor input. Paste events bubble from the editor `<input>` (injected by EditingPlugin) up to the grid; if `ClipboardPlugin` (or any future plugin) calls `preventDefault()` unconditionally, Ctrl+V into a cell editor inserts nothing. Guard the handler with `target.closest('input, textarea, select, [contenteditable]')` and early-return ‚Äî let the browser deliver the event natively. Same applies to any cross-plugin keyboard interception when EditingPlugin is loaded.
25. **For `Ctrl+Z` / `Ctrl+Y` on editable targets, defer to native browser history first** ‚Äî Inputs/textareas/contenteditable elements have their own per-element undo stack. Unconditionally calling `preventDefault()` on Ctrl+Z robs the user of the ability to undo their typing or paste inside an open editor. Pattern (see `UndoRedoPlugin.#deferToNativeHistory`): when target is editable, install a one-shot capture-phase `beforeinput` listener watching for `inputType === 'historyUndo'`/`'historyRedo'` and queue a microtask. If the listener fires, the browser handled it ‚Äî do nothing. If not, the native history is depleted; run the grid's own undo/redo. Return `true` from `onKeyDown` to stop other plugins, but DO NOT call `preventDefault()` ‚Äî the browser default action must be allowed to fire so it can dispatch (or not dispatch) the `beforeinput`. `<select>` is excluded from "editable" because it has no undo history.
